cards.back: A network in which two devices (nodes) are directly connected using a single physical link, allowing data to be transmitted between them
A type of link that allows data to be transmitted in both directions, i.e., both send and receive, at the same time

cards.front: What is the purpose of implementing a duplex link in a network?
cards.back: To increase the bandwidth of the network by allowing data to be transmitted in both directions simultaneously

cards.front: What is a node in a network?
cards.back: A device that connects to other devices in the network, such as a computer, router, or switch

cards.front: What is a point-to-point network with three nodes?
cards.back: A network with three devices (nodes) connected in a linear fashion, where each node is connected to the next one using a duplex link
